---
title: "Examples Week12"
author: "Charles Sandahl"
date: "2026-04-03"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(MASS)
library(ggplot2)
```


```{r}
irisModel = lda(Species~Sepal.Length+Sepal.Width+Petal.Length+Petal.Width,
                   data = iris,
                   prior=c(1/3,1/3,1/3))
irisModel$means
# for the linear combo (will not use probably)
irisModel$scaling
# singular value decomposition, which shows proportion of separation from each
# linear discriminent. First one is LD1, second is LD 2
irisModel$svd
# % of seperation along LD1
48.64^2/(48.64^2+4.58^2)
# % of seperation along LD1
4.58^2/(4.58^2+48.64^2)

# predict the group for a new individual
newFlower = data.frame(Sepal.Length=6,Sepal.Width=3,Petal.Length=5,Petal.Width=2)
predict(object = irisModel, newdata = newFlower)

```
